state.output:
<path>/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/scripts/shared/loop-host.cjs</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: #!/usr/bin/env node
2: // ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
3: // ║ loop-host — mode-switching entry point for deep-improvement runs         ║
4: // ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
5: 'use strict';
6: 
7: /**
8:  * Mode-switching entry point for deep-improvement. Routes between the
9:  * existing `agent-improvement` scoring path and the new `model-benchmark`
10:  * pipeline. This is the seam host that was previously missing
11:  * (deep-improvement has no loop.cjs — orchestration is journal-based).
12:  *
13:  * Backward-compat contract: with `--mode=agent-improvement` OR no
14:  * `--mode` flag, behavior is identical — both route to score-candidate.cjs with
15:  * the same arguments, so the produced score/state output is byte-identical.
16:  *
17:  * Usage:
18:  *   agent-improvement (default):
19:  *     node loop-host.cjs [--mode=agent-improvement] --candidate=<path> [--baseline=<path>] [--output=<path>]
20:  *   model-benchmark:
21:  *     node loop-host.cjs --mode=model-benchmark --profile=<path-or-id> --outputs-dir=<path> \
22:  *        [--output=<path>] [--state-log=<path>] [--label=<string>] [--profiles-dir=<path>] \
23:  *        [--scorer=<pattern|5dim>] [--grader=<noop|mock|llm>]
24:  *   non-dev-ai-system-refine (Lane D):
25:  *     node loop-host.cjs --mode=non-dev-ai-system-refine --packaging-root=<path> \
26:  *        [--live] [--max-iters=<n>] [--fixtures=<a,b>] [--variants=<a,b>] \
27:  *        [--held-out=<a,b>] [--samples=<n>] [--proposer-model=<id>] [--grader-model=<id>]
28:  *
29:  * Unknown --mode values warn to stderr and fall back to agent-improvement.
30:  */
31: 
32: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
33: // 1. IMPORTS
34: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
35: 
36: const path = require('path');
37: const { spawnSync } = require('child_process');
38: const { parseArgs } = require('./parse-args.cjs');
39: 
40: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
41: // 2. CONSTANTS
42: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
43: 
44: const SCRIPTS_ROOT = __dirname;
45: const VALID_MODES = new Set(['agent-improvement', 'model-benchmark', 'skill-benchmark', 'non-dev-ai-system-refine']);
46: 
47: // Lane separation: planInvocation() returns BARE script names (e.g.
48: // 'score-candidate.cjs') to keep the backward-compat identity plan
49: // byte-identical. The real on-disk lane lives in sibling lane dirs relative to
50: // this file (now scripts/shared/). resolveScriptPath maps a bare name to its
51: // lane path at SPAWN time so the plan output never carries lane segments.
52: const LANE_A = new Set([
53:   'score-candidate.cjs',
54:   'generate-profile.cjs',
55:   'rollback-candidate.cjs',
56:   'candidate-lineage.cjs',
57:   'scan-integration.cjs',
58:   'check-mirror-drift.cjs',
59:   'trade-off-detector.cjs',
60:   'benchmark-stability.cjs',
61: ]);
62: const LANE_MODEL_BENCHMARK = new Set([
63:   'run-benchmark.cjs',
64:   'dispatch-model.cjs',
65: ]);
66: // Lane C (skill-benchmark): the orchestrator the run resolves to. Sub-scripts
67: // (router-replay, contamination-lint, d5-connectivity, parse-resource-loads,
68: // score-skill-benchmark, build-report) are required internally by the
69: // orchestrator, so only the entry script needs lane-path resolution here.
70: const LANE_SKILL_BENCHMARK = new Set([
71:   'run-skill-benchmark.cjs',
72: ]);
73: // Lane D (non-dev-ai-system-refine): a thin adapter that spawns the loop
74: // host living WITH the packaging under test (<packaging-root>/benchmark/_loop/loop.py).
75: // The guarded loop logic (frozen scoring surface, independent re-grade,
76: // worktree promote-N, kill-switches) belongs to the packaging, not this skill.
77: const LANE_NON_DEV_AI_SYSTEM = new Set([
78:   'run-non-dev-ai-system.cjs',
79: ]);
80: 
81: // Optional flags loop-host forwards to run-non-dev-ai-system.cjs, in forwarding
82: // order. --packaging-root is required and handled separately. This list is
83: // exactly the surface run-non-dev-ai-system.cjs consumes (--live + its
84: // ENV_FORWARD map + --max-iters).
85: const NON_DEV_AI_SYSTEM_RUN_OPTIONS = [
86:   'live',
87:   'max-iters',
88:   'fixtures',
89:   'variants',
90:   'held-out',
91:   'samples',
92:   'proposer-model',
93:   'grader-model',
94: ];
95: 
96: // Optional flags loop-host forwards to run-skill-benchmark.cjs, in forwarding
97: // order. --skill and --outputs-dir are required and handled separately. This
98: // list is exactly the set run-skill-benchmark.cjs's run() reads; forwarding a
99: // flag the orchestrator never consumes would be silently absorbed and mislead
100: // operators into thinking it has effect.
101: const SKILL_BENCHMARK_RUN_OPTIONS = [
102:   'fixtures-dir',
103:   'output',
104:   'trace-mode',
105:   'advisor-mode',
106:   'scenarios',
107:   'executor',
108:   'playbook-dir',
109: ];
110: 
111: // Single source of truth for the optional model-benchmark flags loop-host
112: // forwards to run-benchmark.cjs (one shared benchmark option schema). Order is
113: // the forwarding order. --profile and --outputs-dir are required and handled
114: // separately. Keep this aligned with run-benchmark.cjs's accepted options.
115: const BENCHMARK_RUN_OPTIONS = [
116:   'output',
117:   'state-log',
118:   'label',
119:   'profiles-dir',
120:   'integration-report',
121:   'scorer',
122:   'grader',
123:   'samples',
124:   'allow-same-family',
125: ];
126: 
127: // The fixture materializer also needs --profiles-dir so a profile passed by ID
128: // (not a path) resolves identically in BOTH steps. --profile and
129: // --outputs-dir are forwarded explicitly; this lists the remaining
130: // materialize-relevant pass-through flags.
131: const BENCHMARK_MATERIALIZE_OPTIONS = [
132:   'profiles-dir',
133: ];
134: 
135: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
136: // 3. HELPERS
137: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
138: 
139: /**
140:  * Map a bare lane script name to its on-disk path at spawn time.
141:  *
142:  * @param {string} scriptName - Bare script name (e.g. 'score-candidate.cjs')
143:  * @returns {string} Absolute path to the resolved lane script
144:  */
145: function resolveScriptPath(scriptName) {
146:   if (LANE_A.has(scriptName)) {
147:     return path.join(SCRIPTS_ROOT, '..', 'agent-improvement', scriptName);
148:   }
149:   if (LANE_MODEL_BENCHMARK.has(scriptName)) {
150:     return path.join(SCRIPTS_ROOT, '..', 'model-benchmark', scriptName);
151:   }
152:   if (LANE_SKILL_BENCHMARK.has(scriptName)) {
153:     return path.join(SCRIPTS_ROOT, '..', 'skill-benchmark', scriptName);
154:   }
155:   if (LANE_NON_DEV_AI_SYSTEM.has(scriptName)) {
156:     return path.join(SCRIPTS_ROOT, '..', 'non-dev-ai-system', scriptName);
157:   }
158:   // Other shared scripts (materialize-benchmark-fixtures, promote-candidate,
159:   // reduce-state, improvement-journal, mutation-coverage) live alongside this file.
160:   return path.join(SCRIPTS_ROOT, scriptName);
161: }
162: 
163: /**
164:  * Resolve a raw --mode value to a valid mode, defaulting unknown values.
165:  *
166:  * @param {string|undefined} rawMode - Raw --mode flag value
167:  * @returns {string} A valid mode, falling back to 'agent-improvement'
168:  */
169: function resolveMode(rawMode) {
170:   if (rawMode === undefined) return 'agent-improvement';
171:   if (VALID_MODES.has(rawMode)) return rawMode;
172:   process.stderr.write(`loop-host: unknown mode '${rawMode}', defaulting to 'agent-improvement'\n`);
173:   return 'agent-improvement';
174: }
175: 
176: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
177: // 4. CORE LOGIC
178: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
179: 
180: /**
181:  * Pure planner: map (mode, args) -> the ordered script invocations to run.
182:  * Planning is separated from execution so the backward-compat identity gate
183:  * the identity gate can assert byte-identical plans for the default and explicit
184:  * agent-improvement routes without spawning anything.
185:  *
186:  * @param {string} mode - Resolved run mode (agent-improvement, model-benchmark, skill-benchmark, non-dev-ai-system-refine)
187:  * @param {object} args - Parsed CLI args keyed by flag name
188:  * @returns {{ ok: true, steps: Array<{script: string, args: string[]}> } | { ok: false, error: string }}
189:  */
190: function planInvocation(mode, args) {
191:   if (mode === 'model-benchmark') {
192:     if (!args.profile || !args['outputs-dir']) {
193:       return { ok: false, error: 'model-benchmark: missing required --profile=<path-or-id> and --outputs-dir=<path>' };
194:     }
195:     // materialize MUST run (and succeed) before run-benchmark, else scoring
196:     // silently produces all-zero "missing-output" results. These scripts use
197:     // space-separated args.
198:     //
199:     // Both steps share one option schema (BENCHMARK_* lists) so forwarding stays
200:     // in sync with run-benchmark.cjs and the materializer, instead of a hand-rolled
201:     // per-flag chain that silently drifts.
202:     // run-benchmark.cjs forwards scorer/grader/integration-report so the journal
203:     // and report.json agree on the requested method; the materializer forwards
204:     // --profiles-dir so a profile-by-ID resolves identically in both steps.
205:     const materializeArgs = ['--profile', args.profile, '--outputs-dir', args['outputs-dir']];
206:     for (const opt of BENCHMARK_MATERIALIZE_OPTIONS) {
207:       if (args[opt] !== undefined) materializeArgs.push(`--${opt}`, String(args[opt]));
208:     }
209:     const benchArgs = ['--profile', args.profile, '--outputs-dir', args['outputs-dir']];
210:     for (const opt of BENCHMARK_RUN_OPTIONS) {
211:       if (args[opt] !== undefined) benchArgs.push(`--${opt}`, String(args[opt]));
212:     }
213:     return {
214:       ok: true,
215:       steps: [
216:         { script: 'materialize-benchmark-fixtures.cjs', args: materializeArgs },
217:         { script: 'run-benchmark.cjs', args: benchArgs },
218:       ],
219:     };
220:   }
221:   if (mode === 'skill-benchmark') {
222:     if (!args.skill || !args['outputs-dir']) {
223:       return { ok: false, error: 'skill-benchmark: missing required --skill=<skill-root-or-id> and --outputs-dir=<path>' };
224:     }
225:     // Single orchestrator step: run-skill-benchmark.cjs internally sequences
226:     // D5-gate -> router-replay -> (optional live) -> trace-parse -> score ->
227:     // report, so the plan stays one step (unlike Lane B's materialize+run pair).
228:     // Lane C scripts use space-separated args.
229:     const skillArgs = ['--skill', args.skill, '--outputs-dir', args['outputs-dir']];
230:     for (const opt of SKILL_BENCHMARK_RUN_OPTIONS) {
231:       if (args[opt] !== undefined) skillArgs.push(`--${opt}`, String(args[opt]));
232:     }
233:     return { ok: true, steps: [{ script: 'run-skill-benchmark.cjs', args: skillArgs }] };
234:   }
235:   if (mode === 'non-dev-ai-system-refine') {
236:     if (!args['packaging-root']) {
237:       return { ok: false, error: 'non-dev-ai-system-refine: missing required --packaging-root=<path>' };
238:     }
239:     // Single adapter step (same single-orchestrator plan shape as Lane C): run-non-dev-ai-system.cjs validates the
240:     // benchmark/_loop/loop.py contract and spawns the packaging's own guarded loop host.
241:     // Lane D scripts use space-separated args. --live is a boolean: forwarded
242:     // bare so the adapter's parser keeps it a flag, not a key/value pair.
243:     const refineArgs = ['--packaging-root', String(args['packaging-root'])];
244:     for (const opt of NON_DEV_AI_SYSTEM_RUN_OPTIONS) {
245:       if (args[opt] === undefined) continue;
246:       if (args[opt] === true) refineArgs.push(`--${opt}`);
247:       else refineArgs.push(`--${opt}`, String(args[opt]));
248:     }
249:     return { ok: true, steps: [{ script: 'run-non-dev-ai-system.cjs', args: refineArgs }] };
250:   }
251:   // agent-improvement (default). score-candidate.cjs uses key=value args.
252:   if (!args.candidate) {
253:     return { ok: false, error: 'agent-improvement: missing required --candidate=<path>' };
254:   }
255:   const scoreArgs = [`--candidate=${args.candidate}`];
256:   if (args.baseline) scoreArgs.push(`--baseline=${args.baseline}`);
257:   if (args.output) scoreArgs.push(`--output=${args.output}`);
258:   return { ok: true, steps: [{ script: 'score-candidate.cjs', args: scoreArgs }] };
259: }

<path>/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/scripts/lib/mirror-sync-verify.cjs</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: // ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
2: // ║ mirror-sync-verify — repo-managed agent mirror sync verifier            ║
3: // ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
4: 'use strict';
5: 
6: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
7: // 1. REQUIRES
8: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
9: 
10: const fs = require('node:fs');
11: const path = require('node:path');
12: 
13: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
14: // 2. CONSTANTS
15: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
16: 
17: const RUNTIME_MIRRORS = Object.freeze([
18:   { runtime: 'opencode', template: '.opencode/agents/{name}.md', format: 'markdown' },
19:   { runtime: 'claude', template: '.claude/agents/{name}.md', format: 'markdown' },
20:   { runtime: 'codex', template: '.codex/agents/{name}.toml', format: 'codex-toml' },
21: ]);
22: 
23: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
24: // 3. HELPERS
25: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
26: 
27: function resolveMirrorPath(repoRoot, template, agentName) {
28:   return path.join(repoRoot, template.replace('{name}', agentName));
29: }
30: 
31: /**
32:  * Strip leading YAML frontmatter from markdown content.
33:  *
34:  * @param {string} content - Raw file content.
35:  * @returns {string} Content with the leading frontmatter block removed.
36:  */
37: function stripFrontmatter(content) {
38:   return String(content || '').replace(/^---[\s\S]*?---\n/, '');
39: }
40: 
41: function extractCodexDeveloperInstructions(content) {
42:   const text = String(content || '');
43:   const tripleSingle = text.match(/developer_instructions\s*=\s*'''([\s\S]*?)'''/);
44:   if (tripleSingle) {
45:     return tripleSingle[1];
46:   }
47:   const tripleDouble = text.match(/developer_instructions\s*=\s*"""([\s\S]*?)"""/);
48:   if (tripleDouble) {
49:     return tripleDouble[1];
50:   }
51:   return null;
52: }
53: 
54: /**
55:  * Extract the agent body from runtime-mirror content by format.
56:  *
57:  * @param {string} content - Raw mirror file content.
58:  * @param {string} [format] - Mirror format ('markdown' or 'codex-toml').
59:  * @returns {string|null} Trimmed agent body, or null if not extractable.
60:  */
61: function extractAgentBody(content, format = 'markdown') {
62:   if (format === 'codex-toml') {
63:     const body = extractCodexDeveloperInstructions(content);
64:     return body === null ? null : body.trim();
65:   }
66:   return stripFrontmatter(content).trim();
67: }
68: 
69: function normalizeRuntimeSpecificText(body) {
70:   return String(body || '')
71:     .replace(/\.(?:opencode|claude)\/agents\/\*\.md/g, '<runtime-agent-path>')
72:     .replace(/\.codex\/agents\/\*\.toml/g, '<runtime-agent-path>')
73:     // Agent-file references include literal placeholders like `<name>`, so the
74:     // name class allows < and >. This normalizes the per-runtime path AND its
75:     // extension together — keeping the extension scoped to the agents-path
76:     // construct, so a real body difference (e.g. "config.md" vs "config.toml")
77:     // outside an agents path still registers as drift.
78:     .replace(/\.(?:opencode|claude)\/agents\/[A-Za-z0-9_<>-]+\.md/g, '<runtime-agent-file>')
79:     .replace(/\.codex\/agents\/[A-Za-z0-9_<>-]+\.toml/g, '<runtime-agent-file>')
80:     // Each mirror describes itself ("this runtime's mirror; the canonical source
81:     // lives in .opencode/agents/") in a parenthetical the .opencode canonical
82:     // omits. That is legitimate per-runtime self-description, not body drift, so
83:     // drop the whole clause before comparing.
84:     .replace(/\(this runtime.s mirror;[^)]*\)/gi, '')
85:     .replace(/\r\n/g, '\n')
86:     .replace(/[ \t]+$/gm, '')
87:     .trim();
88: }
89: 
90: function tokenizeBody(body) {
91:   const normalized = normalizeRuntimeSpecificText(body).toLowerCase();
92:   return new Set(normalized.match(/[a-z0-9_./*-]{3,}/g) || []);
93: }
94: 
95: /**
96:  * Compare two agent bodies by normalized token sets for drift.
97:  *
98:  * @param {string} expectedBody - Canonical expected body.
99:  * @param {string} actualBody - Mirror body to compare against.
100:  * @returns {Object} Comparison with matches flag and missing/unexpected tokens.
101:  */
102: function compareBodyTokens(expectedBody, actualBody) {
103:   const expectedTokens = tokenizeBody(expectedBody);
104:   const actualTokens = tokenizeBody(actualBody);
105:   const missingTokens = [...expectedTokens].filter((token) => !actualTokens.has(token)).sort();
106:   const unexpectedTokens = [...actualTokens].filter((token) => !expectedTokens.has(token)).sort();
107: 
108:   return {
109:     matches: missingTokens.length === 0 && unexpectedTokens.length === 0,
110:     missingTokenCount: missingTokens.length,
111:     unexpectedTokenCount: unexpectedTokens.length,
112:     missingTokens: missingTokens.slice(0, 20),
113:     unexpectedTokens: unexpectedTokens.slice(0, 20),
114:   };
115: }
116: 
117: /**
118:  * Infer the agent name from a mirror file path (basename without extension).
119:  *
120:  * @param {string} filePath - Path to a runtime-mirror agent file.
121:  * @returns {string} Agent name derived from the file basename.
122:  */
123: function inferAgentNameFromPath(filePath) {
124:   const ext = path.extname(filePath);
125:   return path.basename(filePath, ext);
126: }
127: 
128: /**
129:  * Resolve the per-runtime mirror paths for an agent name.
130:  *
131:  * @param {string} agentName - Agent name to expand into mirror paths.
132:  * @param {string} [repoRoot] - Repository root for absolute path resolution.
133:  * @returns {Array<Object>} Mirror descriptors with runtime, path, absolutePath, format.
134:  */
135: function runtimePaths(agentName, repoRoot = process.cwd()) {
136:   return RUNTIME_MIRRORS.map((mirror) => ({
137:     runtime: mirror.runtime,
138:     path: mirror.template.replace('{name}', agentName),
139:     absolutePath: resolveMirrorPath(repoRoot, mirror.template, agentName),
140:     format: mirror.format,
141:   }));
142: }
143: 
144: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
145: // 4. CORE LOGIC
146: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
147: 
148: /**
149:  * Verify that all repo-managed runtime mirrors of an agent are present and in sync.

1: #!/usr/bin/env node

3: // ║ score-model-variant — decoupled 5-dimension model-benchmark scorer       ║

5: 'use strict';

7: /**
8:  * Decoupled 5-dimension scorer for the deep-improvement model-benchmark
9:  * mode. Ported from the eval-loop score-variant and decoupled from the
10:  * fixture-JSON-file assumption per the 002 research (iteration 3):
11:  *
12:  *   - PUBLIC API takes PRIMITIVE CRITERIA + an absolute `cwd`, never a fixture
13:  *     file path. The caller (loop-host / run-benchmark adapter) extracts the
14:  *     criteria from its profile/fixture and passes them as data.
15:  *   - Internally it synthesizes an in-memory "virtual fixture" (with an absolute
16:  *     scope.cwd) and writes it to a temp JSON only so the proven deterministic
17:  *     check subprocesses + grader can consume it unchanged. det-check scripts
18:  *     resolve `path.resolve(PACKET_ROOT, scope.cwd)`, which is a no-op for an
19:  *     absolute cwd — so they are decoupled from this module's location.
20:  *   - D4 grader is pluggable via buildGraderFn(graderKind): 'llm' (real claude),
21:  *     'mock' (deterministic stub), or 'noop' (D4=1.0). Default 'mock'.
22:  *
23:  * Seam contract:
24:  *   score({ candidateId, candidateHash, outputText, criteria, rubric?, cwd,
25:  *           graderKind?, graderMode?, mockMode? })

18: // without any real CLI call. Dependency-free (Node stdlib only).

  Line 38: `deep-context` is an iterative multi-model context-gathering loop. It sweeps the existing repository in parallel with a configurable pool of executor seats and synthesizes a reuse-first Context Report. The report ships verified `file:symbol` pointers, not source bodies, so the consumer pulls code just-in-time. It runs the same scope through every seat at once, merges findings by cross-executor agreement and stops only when five convergence signals say enough context has been gathered. It does not plan, review or implement. Hand the report to `/speckit:plan` and let the planner decide.

  Line 96: Five signals decide when the loop has gathered enough context. Three are blocking guards: `sliceCoverage` (0.70), `agreementRate` (0.50) and `relevanceFloor` (0.50). If any guard fails, the decision is STOP_BLOCKED and the loop continues with a recovery focus. Two are weighted contributors: `reuseCatalogCoverage` (weight 0.30, the highest) and `dependencyCompleteness` (weight 0.10). The host also runs a saturation check: new agreement-eligible findings per iteration must fall below the convergence threshold (default 0.10) for two consecutive sweeps before the loop is allowed to stop. A high composite score cannot buy past a failed blocking guard.
